title: Training
selectProject: Select a project to configure training
noProjects: No projects available. Create a project first.

params.title: Training Parameters
params.modelHint: HuggingFace repo ID or local path (e.g. mlx-community/Llama-3.2-3B-Instruct-4bit)
params.itersHint: Number of training iterations. More iterations = longer training but potentially better results.
params.batchSizeHint: Samples per batch. Lower if you run out of memory.
params.loraLayersHint: Number of layers to apply LoRA. More layers = more parameters to train.
params.loraRankHint: Rank of LoRA matrices. Higher rank = more expressive but more memory.
params.learningRateHint: Step size for optimization. Too high → unstable, too low → slow.
params.seedHint: Set to 0 for random. Use a fixed seed for reproducible results.
params.loraScaleHint: Adjustment magnitude. Higher values = more obvious changes to the model.
params.loraDropoutHint: Random drop ratio. Increase slightly to prevent overfitting (memorizing data).
params.maxSeqLengthHint: Max length per training sample. Choose based on your data's average length.
params.gradCheckpointHint: Saves memory but slows training. Enable when running low on memory.
params.stepsPerEvalHint: Check model performance every N iterations.
params.stepsPerReportHint: Update training metrics on screen every N iterations.
params.valBatchesHint: How many batches to use for validation. More = more accurate but slower.
params.optimizerHint: Algorithm for weight updates. Adam is the most common default.
params.gradAccumulationStepsHint: Accumulate N batches before updating. Effectively multiplies batch size without extra memory.
params.saveEveryHint: Save adapter checkpoint every N iterations. Protects against losing progress.
params.maskPromptHint: Only compute loss on completions, ignore the prompt part. Improves SFT quality.

method.title: Training Method
method.loraDesc: Low-Rank Adaptation. Efficient fine-tuning by training small adapter matrices. Recommended for most use cases.
method.doraDesc: Weight-Decomposed Low-Rank Adaptation. Enhanced LoRA with better performance, slightly more compute.
method.fullDesc: Full parameter fine-tuning. Trains all model weights. Requires significantly more memory.
method.hint: Select a method after choosing model and dataset.
method.loraScaleStrategyHint: Controls how LoRA adapter outputs are scaled before being added to the base model weights. Standard uses alpha/r; rsLoRA uses alpha/√r — which keeps the effective learning signal stable at higher ranks, improving training consistency when rank > 32.
method.scaleRsloraHint: Recommended for rank ≥ 32. Stabilises training at high rank — results may be more consistent but training time is slightly higher.

noLog: Training log will appear here once started.

section.selectModelHint: Choose the base large model you want to fine-tune (i.e., teach new knowledge or skills). The base model is the starting point — training adds new capabilities on top without modifying the original model. Supports HuggingFace repo IDs or local paths. For first-time users, quantized models under mlx-community/ are recommended for fast, low-memory training.
section.selectDatasetHint: Select the training dataset you generated in the Data Prep stage. The dataset determines what the model will learn — quality matters more than quantity. If you have multiple versions, choose the one with the most samples and the highest quality score (Q-A/B).
section.methodHint: Choose your fine-tuning approach: LoRA is the most popular method — trains only a small set of adapter parameters, fast and low-memory, suitable for nearly all use cases; DoRA is an enhanced LoRA with slightly better results but slower; Full trains all model parameters for maximum performance but requires enormous memory — not recommended for most users. When in doubt, choose LoRA.
section.paramsHint: Training parameters determine the rhythm and depth of the model's learning. Beginners can directly select the preset options on the left, which are already optimized. If you want to dive deeper: Epochs control how many times the model sees the data, Batch Size controls how many samples it sees at once, and Learning Rate controls the step size of each learning update. Click below to expand advanced parameters.

selectModelHint: Select a model to start training
invalidModelPath: No valid MLX model files found at this path
noDataset: No training dataset found. Generate one in Data Preparation first.

eta.title: Estimated Time Left
eta.hint: Estimated time to completion (ETA) based on current iteration speed. Early estimates may be inaccurate and will stabilize as training progresses. Treat as a rough guide — actual time may vary with system load.

health.title: Training Health
health.hint: Real-time overview of training health. Green = all good; Yellow = something needs attention (e.g., loss fluctuating, temperature elevated); Red = serious issue detected (e.g., out of memory, training stalled). Check the Smart Alerts section below for details.
health.hintNormal: Training is stable. You can keep waiting.
health.hintLossFluctuating: Loss is slightly fluctuating, which is usually normal.
health.hintLossRising: Loss is trending upward. Check data quality or learning rate.
health.hintOutOfMemory: Memory pressure detected. Reduce batch size or enable grad checkpoint.
health.hintError: Training error detected. Check logs to locate the issue.

trend.title: Loss Trend
trend.hint: Real-time direction of loss change: consistently decreasing = the model is still learning effectively; stable = approaching convergence, training nearly done; consistently increasing = something may be wrong — stop and try a lower learning rate.
trend.warning: Fluctuating — keep an eye on it

alerts.title: Smart Alerts
alerts.none: No active alerts. Training is stable.

memory.title: Memory pressure detected
memory.detail: Training hit memory limits and may fail soon.
memory.action: Lower batch size or max sequence length, then restart training.

runtime.title: Runtime exception detected
runtime.detail: An exception signature appeared in recent logs.
runtime.action: Open training logs and fix the reported script/model issue first.

thermal.title: Thermal throttling risk
thermal.detail: Device temperature may be limiting training speed.
thermal.action: Close heavy apps or pause briefly to cool down, then continue.

stalled.title: Progress appears stalled
stalled.detail: No new iterations were observed for a while.
stalled.action: Check I/O and logs. If still stuck, stop and restart with safer params.

lossRising.title: Loss is rising
lossRising.detail: Recent train loss trend moved upward noticeably.
lossRising.action: Review data quality and lower learning rate if needed.

lossStopped: Training was stopped. Not enough data to draw a loss curve yet.
lossNoData: Not enough data to draw a loss curve yet.
invalidModelError: No valid model found at the selected path. Cannot start training.

summary.title: Training Summary
summary.durationHint: Total time taken for this training run. Use this as a reference when tuning parameters — if it takes too long, consider fewer iterations or a quick preset.
summary.trainLossHint: Training Loss measures how well the model has learned the training data. Lower is better, but too low may indicate overfitting — memorizing data instead of truly understanding it.
summary.valLossHint: Validation Loss measures how well the model generalizes to unseen data. Slightly higher than training loss is normal. If it's much higher, overfitting may have occurred — try reducing iterations.
summary.lossImproveHint: Percentage change in loss from start to finish. ↓ means loss decreased (training was effective); larger values indicate more improvement. If it shows ↑ increase, consider adjusting the learning rate or reviewing data quality.

importDataset.title: Import Custom Dataset
importDataset.desc: Import your own pre-formatted JSONL files directly into the training stage, bypassing Data Preparation.
importDataset.trainRequired: Required — training samples (one JSON per line)
importDataset.validOptional: Optional — validation samples, same format. Recommended for tracking training quality.
importDataset.errorNoTrain: train.jsonl not found in the selected folder.

validation.needModel: Please select a training model first
validation.needMethod: Please select a training method first
